title: Roasted Cauliflower, Miso & Chestnut Soup
summary: Caramelised cauliflower, miso and chestnuts make up this rich and creamy, umami soup that’s easy enough to whip up on a weeknight but is also perfect for a special occasion.

ingredients.label: 5 Garlic Cloves (Plus More for the Garlic Toast)
ingredients.name: Garlic Clove

ingredients.label: 5 Garlic Cloves (Plus More for the Garlic Toast)
ingredients.name: Garlic

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Olive Oil or Vegan Butter
ingredients.name: Olive Oil

method.description: Preheat the oven to 180°C. Cut the cauliflower into large pieces and place on a baking tray. Add the garlic cloves in their skins to the tray. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Roast for 20-25 mins until golden and starting to char and the garlic is soft.

When cool enough to handle, remove the skins from the garlic cloves. Add the cooked cauliflower to a large blender along with the peeled garlic, white miso, lesser amount of oat milk, chestnuts, nutritional yeast, olive oil, water and stock cube. Blend until smooth and season well with salt and pepper. Alternatively, place everything into a large pot and use an immersion blender to blend until smooth.
Heat the soup up on the stove in a large pot and add more oat milk or water to thin down if needed.
Toast the bread and rub it with garlic cloves and then drizzle with a little olive oil to make garlic bread. Top with vegan cream cheese and a drizzle of crispy chilli oil, if you like.

chefs.name: Christina Soteriou
chefs.summary: Christina (@christinasots) is a plant-based chef and recipe developer with a love for the Mediterranean flavours of her home, Cyprus. With an equal balance of healthy, flavourful and easy to make, there are no compromises here!
